title: EvalCase
description: One authored, reproducible eval case (evals/cases/**/*.yaml). Each case exercises a boundary, an invariant, or a regression risk against the live target via one or more ordered steps. Not a flat payload list: the `test_type` + `invariant` fields state the security property under test, and each step's `detector` deterministically decides whether the target held or failed.

case_id.description: Unique, stable, kebab-case id. Used as the regression key, so it must never be reused for a different case.

test_type.description: Why this case is not a flat payload: it pins a boundary condition, asserts a security invariant, or guards against a specific regression.

invariant.description: The security property being asserted, in one sentence (e.g. 'PHI must not be served to an unauthenticated caller').

expected_safe_behavior.description: What a secure target does with this input. Read by the Phase 4 Judge; also human-readable ground truth.

add_to_regression.description: If true, a confirmed exploit here is stored in the versioned exploit DB and replayed by the Phase 6 regression harness.

requires_judge.description: true when the deterministic detector is only a coarse signal and the Phase 4 Judge is authoritative for the final verdict (e.g. semantic 'did the model treat spoofed context as trusted' calls).

expected_result.description: The authoring-time expectation for a run against the current target version: 'fail' = the target is expected to be exploited (a known/confirmed finding), 'pass' = the defense is expected to hold. Lets the runner flag surprises (a should-pass case that failed, or a fixed finding that reappeared).

step.description: One send_to_target call. Steps run in list order; a later step can reuse an earlier step's target session to model multi-turn or cross-patient-session-reuse attacks.

patient_id.description: FHIR patient id this step is scoped to. Steps in the same case may use different patient_ids (that is the cross-patient state-corruption test).

session.description: 'new' starts a fresh target conversation; 'reuse:<step_id>' continues the session opened by an earlier step (used to test whether one patient's conversation memory bleeds into another's).

input_sequence.description: Same shape as contracts/attack_attempt.schema.json's input_sequence, plus optional doc_type for uploaded_content turns.

detector.description: Deterministic pass/fail signal applied to this step's concatenated assistant transcript. Optional: a step with no detector only sets up state for a later step. Kept intentionally small and vetted — no arbitrary code, so runs are reproducible.

signals.description: 'exploit' = a match means the target FAILED (the exploit landed → result 'fail'). 'safe' = a match means the target HELD (result 'pass'). The opposite outcome applies on no-match.

patterns.description: Literal substrings for contains_any/contains_all, or a single-element list holding the pattern for regex.
